evidence: "This is the best of the Star Trek from an arc and storytelling perspective."
"I really enjoy Peter Capaldi as the doctor."
"It was intentionally character driven, not plot driven."
"This episode really has the best of headwriter Steven Moffat's style of storytelling."
"This season has good companions... but doesn't quite have the charisma of some other doctors."
"This is a very engaging and informative course on the development of the Cathedral."
"Superb writing with a few clunkers here and there."
"The acting is very strong, and some individual scenes are just stellar."
"I really enjoy Peter Capaldi as the doctor."
"It has humor, but it's an emotionally wrenching and very beautiful story about a person who suffered from her beloved father's alcoholism."
"This is a classic. I just love it."
"This one is for teens and adults."
"It pulls the story from the 'apocraphal' books of Enoch and Jubilees... It makes for a very good story, and makes you think about trying to understand a calling (and getting it wrong)."

summary: The user has a strong preference for sci-fi and drama genres, particularly enjoying character-driven narratives with complex storytelling. They value high production quality and emotional resonance in their viewing experiences, often reflecting on the depth and impact of the stories they engage with.
